authors: Sinclair NC, McDermott HJ, Bulluss KJ, Fallon JB, Perera T, Xu SS, Brown P, Thevathasan W
title: Subthalamic Nucleus Deep Brain Stimulation Evokes Resonant Neural Activity
study_question: Does DBS evoke a focal, recordable neural response in the subthalamic nucleus?
population_assessed: 14 Parkinson disease and 5 essential tremor patients undergoing awake DBS implantation surgery.
follow_up_duration: 3-586 days postoperatively (mean 390 days); one patient retested 560 days.
outcome_measures: Evoked resonant neural activity amplitude, clinical motor performance via UPDRS, electrode position localization.
inclusion_criteria: Patients undergoing awake DBS implantation surgery for Parkinson disease or essential tremor.
answer_to_study_question: STN-DBS evokes large-amplitude resonant neural activity focal to dorsal STN, correlating with improved clinical outcomes.
